% Generated by praxis/pillars/conjectures - do not edit by hand.
\newcommand{\paperConjectures}{%
\subsection{Standing conjectures}
We gather the paper's falsifiable conjectures in one place; each is discussed where it arises. They are bets about reachability and cost, not theorems, and each names what would refute it.
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Linear consensus, logarithmic interference.} Learning a corpus as a consensus mean costs resources linearly in the patterns expressed, while composing it by harmonic interference costs only logarithmically, so the coverage-versus-resources slope a basis buys is measurable and basis-dependent.~\cite{kaplan2020}
  \item \textbf{Reachable, not merely representable.} Across architectures of comparable parameter count and compute there exist task and data regimes where generalization error is separated, because the prior sets which solutions stochastic gradient descent can reach, not only which functions exist in the closure.~\cite{telgarsky2016,frankle2019lottery}
  \item \textbf{Lottery engineering.} A geometric prior makes good sparse subnetworks reachable by stochastic gradient descent directly rather than only by train-then-prune, and a rotating population of such subnetworks (mixture-of-widths) approaches full-width performance at lower average active width.~\cite{frankle2019lottery}
\end{itemize}
}
